message:
feat(shadow): implement signal handling, deep scan scheduling, and PID locking

Upgrade the shadow mode daemon with proper Unix signal handling and
operational robustness:

Signal handling (Unix):
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: graceful stop via atomic flag, checked between
  iterations and during interruptible sleep
- SIGHUP: trigger config reload + immediate forced scan
- SIGUSR1: trigger immediate forced scan without reload
- ShadowSignalState: static atomic struct for lock-free signal
  communication between signal handlers and the main loop

Deep scan scheduling:
- New --deep-interval flag (default 3600s, 0 disables) for periodic
  deep scans independent of the regular quick scan interval
- Deep scan deadline tracked via Instant; resets after each deep scan
- force_deep flag threaded through run_shadow_iteration and
  apply_shadow_plan_args to pass --deep to the child process

PID file locking:
- run_shadow_background checks for existing shadow observer PID before
  spawning, returning LockError if already running (prevents duplicate
  daemons)
- cleanup_shadow_pid_if_owned removes PID file on graceful shutdown
  only if the current process still owns it

Interruptible sleep:
- shadow_sleep_with_interrupt polls atomics in 1-second steps so
  SIGTERM/SIGUSR1 are honored promptly rather than waiting the full
  scan interval

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
